basic_details.job_title: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 – 1979 Vacancies for TGT, JSA, IT Assistant & Other Posts (Advertisement No. 03/2026)
basic_details.short_information: Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) has released Advertisement No. 03/2026, inviting online applications for 1979 vacancies across 25 posts in multiple departments of the Government of NCT of Delhi, including Forensic Science Laboratory, Directorate of Education, New Delhi Municipal Council, Information Technology Department, Labour Department, and Department of Delhi Archives. The online application window opens on 16 June 2026 (12:00 Noon) and the last date to apply is 15 July 2026 (11:59 PM). Applications must be submitted only through the official portal at https://dsssbonli

application_fee.details: The application fee is ₹100 (One Hundred only). Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Serviceman categories are fully exempted from paying the fee. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular civil employment under the Central Government or Government of NCT of Delhi after availing ESM reservation benefits are not eligible for fee exemption. | Payment must be made only through SBI ePay. No other payment mode will be accepted, and fees paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: Post Code(s) Age Limit 21/26 to 31/26, 45/26 18 – 27 years 32/26, 33/26 Not exceeding 30 years 34/26 Below 27 years 35/26 20 – 32 years 36/26 18 – 37 years 37/26, 38/26 18 – 32 years 39/26, 40/26 Below 30 years 41/26, 42/26, 43/26, 44/26 Not exceeding 30 years

qualification.details: Candidates must be citizens of India and must meet post-specific educational qualifications and age limits as on the cut-off date of 15 July 2026. | JSA (Biology) – 21/26: Master's degree in Zoology, Botany, Microbiology, Anthropology, Biology, Human Biology, Life Science, Biochemistry, Genetics, Biotechnology, Molecular Biology, or Forensic Science with Zoology or Botany as a subject in all three years of B.Sc.; OR B.E./B.Tech in Biotechnology. | JSA (Ballistics) – 22/26: Master's degree in Physics, Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, or Forensic Science with Physics or Mathematics as a subject across all three B.Sc. years. | JSA (Chemistry) – 23/26: Master's degree in Chemistry, Toxicology, Biochemistry, or Forensic Science with Chemistry as a B.Sc.-level subject. | JSA (Lie-Detection) – 24/26: Master's degree in Psychology or Criminology. | JSA (Documents) – 25/26 & JSA (Finger Print) – 30/26: Master's degree in Physics or Chemistry, or Forensic Science with Physics or Chemistry studied for at least two years/four semesters at B.Sc. level. | JSA (HRD/QC) – 26/26: Master's degree in Physics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Chemistry, Botany, Zoology, Forensic Science, Psychology, Toxicology, or Biotechnology/Genetics; OR two-year PG Diploma in Business Administration/Management (HR specialization); OR MCA; OR B.Tech in Computer Science, Electronics, IT, ECE, or Instrumentation. | JSA (Photo) – 27/26: Master's degree in Physics, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Information Technology, or Forensic Science; OR B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence. | JSA (Cyber Forensic) – 28/26: Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Application, IT, Physics (with Electronics/Cyber Security specialization); OR B.E./B.Tech in CSE, IT, ECE, or Electrical & Electronics Engineering. | JSA (Crime Scene/DMFU) – 29/26: Master's degree in Zoology, Botany, Anthropology, Microbiology, Chemistry, Physics, Toxicology, Biochemistry, or Forensic Science. | JSA (Physics) – 31/26: Master's degree in Physics or Forensic Science with Physics studied for at least two years/four semesters at B.Sc. level. | Technical Assistant (Hindi) – 32/26: Master's degree in Hindi (with English as a compulsory/elective subject or medium at degree level), or in English (with Hindi), or in any other subject under prescribed Hindi/English combinations; AND a recognized Diploma/Certificate in Hindi–English translation or two years' translation experience in Central/State/UT government offices. | Assistant Archivist Grade-I – 33/26: Master's degree in History with a paper in Modern Indian History; AND a Diploma in Archives and Record Management. Desirable: Certificate course in Microsoft Office. | I.T. Assistant Grade-A – 34/26: 12th Standard pass from a recognized institution/board; AND a typing speed of not less than 8000 key depressions per hour (26.67 WPM) in English or Hindi, with qualification in the Aptitude Test. Desirable: one-year recognized Certificate Course in Data Preparation & Computer Software. | Fitter Grade-II – 35/26: Certificate in the trade of Plumber from a recognized institution; AND one year practical experience in handling water distribution networks of CI and GI pipes. | Lift Operator – 36/26: Matric/Higher Secondary; AND ITI certificate in the trade of Electrician/Wireman. | Asstt. Operator cum Mechanic (A/C) – 37/26: Matric/Higher Secondary with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics; AND ITI in Refrigeration and Air Conditioning. Essential experience: minimum two years in Air Conditioning in a reputed firm/undertaking. | Sub Station Apprentice – 38/26: Matric; AND National Trade Certificate of two years' duration in the trade of Electrician awarded by NCTVT, Govt. of India. | Data Processing Assistant Grade 'A' – 39/26: Degree from a recognized university; OR three-year Diploma in Computer Engineering with one year of EDP experience; OR Degree in Computer Engineering. Additionally, a typing speed of at least 8000 key depressions per hour. | Data Entry Operator Grade 'B' – 40/26: Degree from a recognized university; OR three-year Diploma in Computer Engineering/Digital Electronics/ECE with computer application as a subject; AND typing speed of at least 8000 key depressions per hour, with qualification in the Aptitude Test. | TGT (Computer Science) – 41/26: BCA from a recognized university; OR Graduation in Computer Science (computer science as main subject in all years); OR BE/B.Tech in CS/IT; OR Graduation in any subject with 'A' level course from DOEACC. | Domestic Science Teacher – 42/26: Bachelor's degree in Domestic Science/Home Science; AND B.Ed with Domestic Science/Home Science as a teaching subject. Desirable: Hindi passed at secondary level. | TGT (Special Education Teacher) – 43/26: Graduation degree; AND B.Ed (Special Education) or B.Ed (General) with one/two-year Diploma in Special Education, or other specified RCI-recognized qualifications; AND CTET (Elementary Stage); AND RCI registration. SSA resource persons get age relaxation equivalent to years served. Women candidates also get a general age relaxation of 10 years. | Special Educator (Primary) – 44/26: XIIth passed with D.Ed Special Education (two years) or one-year DSE, or specified RCI-recognized qualifications (DCBR, PGDCBR, MRW, Junior Diploma in Teaching the Deaf, etc.); AND CTET (Primary Stage); AND RCI registration. | Electrical Overseer/Sub-Inspector – 45/26: Three-year Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering; AND one year experience (for Diploma holders) in electrical engineering practice. | Note: For Post Code 34/26 (I.T. Assistant Grade-A), age relaxation for government servants applies only to GNCT of Delhi servants as per the Recruitment Rules.

how_to_apply: The application process for DSSSB Recruitment 2026 is entirely online. No applications sent by post, by hand, or by email will be accepted.
Visit the official DSSSB registration and application portal at https://dsssbonline.nic.in.

If you are a new user, complete the one-time registration process. Note down your User ID and password — the same credentials are to be used for all future DSSSB examinations; do not register multiple times.
Log in using your registered credentials and select the relevant post code from Advertisement No. 03/2026.
Fill in all fields of the online application form accurately. Once submitted, no change or correction — including change of category — will be permitted under any circumstances.

Upload a recent, clear colour photograph (plain white or off-white background, full face, front view, eyes open) and your signature. Blurred or illegible photographs/signatures will result in rejection.
Pay the application fee of ₹100 through SBI ePay (exempted categories need not pay).
Submit the form and take a printout for your own records. Keep the registration number and application number safely.
The application portal will be live from 16 June 2026 (12:00 Noon) until 15 July 2026 (11:59 PM), after which the link will be disabled.

content_sections.heading: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: The last date to submit the online application is 15 July 2026 . Eligibility conditions including age, educational qualification, and experience will be determined as on this cut-off date. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date to avoid server congestion in the final hours.

content_sections.heading: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The 1979 vacancies are distributed across 25 post codes (21/26 to 45/26). The table below lists all posts with department and total vacancies. The largest single vacancy pool is for TGT (Computer Science) with 675 posts , followed by Special Educator (Primary) with 450 posts and JSA (Cyber Forensic) with 81 posts . PwBD-included vacancies total 84; ESM-included vacancies total 42; MSP-included vacancies total 8.

content_sections.heading: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must be citizens of India and must meet post-specific educational qualifications and age limits as on the cut-off date of 15 July 2026 . JSA (Biology) – 21/26: Master's degree in Zoology, Botany, Microbiology, Anthropology, Biology, Human Biology, Life Science, Biochemistry, Genetics, Biotechnology, Molecular Biology, or Forensic Science with Zoology or Botany as a subject in all three years of B.Sc.; OR B.E./B.Tech in Biotechnology. JSA (Ballistics) – 22/26: Master's degree in Physics, Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, or Forensic Science with Physics or Mathematics as a subject across all three B.Sc. years. JSA (Chemistry) – 23/26: Master's degree in Chemistry, Toxicology, Biochemistry, or Forensic Science with Chemistry as a B.Sc.-level subject. JSA (Lie-Detection) – 24/26: Master's degree in Psychology or Criminology. JSA (Documents) – 25/26 & JSA (Finger Print) – 30/26: Master's degree in Physics or Chemistry, or Forensic Science with Physics or Chemistry studied for at least two years/four semesters at B.Sc. level. JSA (HRD/QC) – 26/26: Master's degree in Physics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Chemistry, Botany, Zoology, Forensic Science, Psychology, Toxicology, or Biotechnology/Genetics; OR two-year PG Diploma in Business Administration/Management (HR specialization); OR MCA; OR B.Tech in Computer Science, Electronics, IT, ECE, or Instrumentation. JSA (Photo) – 27/26: Master's degree in Physics, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Information Technology, or Forensic Science; OR B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence. JSA (Cyber Forensic) – 28/26: Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Application, IT, Physics (with Electronics/Cyber Security specialization); OR B.E./B.Tech in CSE, IT, ECE, or Electrical & Electronics Engineering. JSA (Crime Scene/DMFU) – 29/26: Master's degree in Zoology, Botany, Anthropology, Microbiology, Chemistry, Physics, Toxicology, Biochemistry, or Forensic Science. JSA (Physics) – 31/26: Master's degree in Physics or Forensic Science with Physics studied for at least two years/four semesters at B.Sc. level. Technical Assistant (Hindi) – 32/26: Master's degree in Hindi (with English as a compulsory/elective subject or medium at degree level), or in English (with Hindi), or in any other subject under prescribed Hindi/English combinations; AND a recognized Diploma/Certificate in Hindi–English translation or two years' translation experience in Central/State/UT government offices. Assistant Archivist Grade-I – 33/26: Master's degree in History with a paper in Modern Indian History; AND a Diploma in Archives and Record Management. Desirable: Certificate course in Microsoft Office. I.T. Assistant Grade-A – 34/26: 12th Standard pass from a recognized institution/board; AND a typing speed of not less than 8000 key depressions per hour (26.67 WPM) in English or Hindi, with qualification in the Aptitude Test. Desirable: one-year recognized Certificate Course in Data Preparation & Computer

content_sections.heading: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee is ₹100 (One Hundred only) . Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Serviceman categories are fully exempted from paying the fee. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular civil employment under the Central Government or Government of NCT of Delhi after availing ESM reservation benefits are not eligible for fee exemption. Payment must be made only through SBI ePay . No other payment mode will be accepted, and fees paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection will be made through a Computer Based Written Examination under one of three examination schemes depending on the post applied for. One Tier General (I-T-G) — Post Codes 34/26, 39/26, 40/26: Duration: 2 hours | 200 MCQs | 200 marks. Five sections of 40 marks each: General Awareness, General Intelligence & Reasoning Ability, Arithmetical & Numerical Ability, Hindi Language & Comprehension, and English Language & Comprehension. Final merit is based on written examination marks only. One Tier Technical (I-T-T) — Post Codes 21/26 to 31/26, 32/26, 33/26, 35/26 to 38/26, 45/26: Duration: 2 hours | 200 MCQs | 200 marks. Section A: 100 marks covering General Awareness, Reasoning, Arithmetic, Hindi, and English (20 marks each). Section B: 100 marks on the domain subject for the post concerned. Minimum qualifying marks apply only to Section B; combined marks of both sections determine the final merit list. One Tier Technical/Teaching (I-T-T) — Post Codes 41/26, 42/26, 43/26, 44/26: Duration: 2 hours | 200 MCQs | 200 marks. Section A: 100 marks (same five general subjects, 20 marks each). Section B: 100 marks from subject-specific content including teaching methodology/B.Ed. Minimum qualifying marks apply only to Section B; combined marks of both sections determine final merit. Minimum Qualifying Marks: Negative marking applies: 0.25 marks will be deducted for each wrong MCQ answer. Skill Test, Aptitude Test, Trade Test, or Physical Endurance Test may be conducted additionally as per job requirements. Marks will be normalized (if required) using the Score Normalisation Based on Deviation Method formula published by DSSSB vide Notice No. 10(271)/Sec.Cell/DSSSB/18/989 dated 11.07.2018.

content_sections.heading: DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: TGT posts (Post Codes 41/26, 42/26, 43/26) carry the highest pay scale at Pay Level 7 (₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400) .

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: The application process for DSSSB Recruitment 2026 is entirely online. No applications sent by post, by hand, or by email will be accepted. The application portal will be live from 16 June 2026 (12:00 Noon) until 15 July 2026 (11:59 PM) , after which the link will be disabled.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for DSSSB 2026 should be careful about a few common pitfalls in the online application process. Photo and signature uploads are a frequent reason for rejection — ensure the image files are clear, correctly sized, and within the file-size limit specified on the portal. Blurred or illegible uploads will cause outright rejection of candidature. If the payment gateway shows an error after fee deduction, do not immediately retry; wait for a few hours to check whether the payment was credited before attempting again through the portal's payment history section. Use an updated browser (Chrome or Firefox preferred) and avoid mobile browsers for form submission, as they may not render all fields correctly. Save your User ID and password immediately after registration and store your application number carefully — all future communications from DSSSB will reference these. Avoid applying on the last day; DSSSB expressly states it will not be responsible for inability to submit due to heavy server load near the closing date.

faq.question: Q1. What is DSSSB Advertisement No. 03/2026 and how many vacancies are there?
faq.answer: DSSSB Advertisement No. 03/2026 (dated 29/05/2026) invites online applications for 1,979 vacancies across 25 post codes in various departments of the Government of NCT of Delhi.

faq.question: Q2. When does the online application window open and close?
faq.answer: Online applications open on 16 June 2026 (12:00 noon) and close on 15 July 2026 (11:59 PM). The eligibility cut‑off date is 15 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. Where do I apply and download the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: Apply online at https://dsssbonline.nic.in. Notification PDF and details are on https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in (official notification: https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in/sites/default/files/DSSSB/circulars-orders/final_advt-03-2026_1.pdf).

faq.question: Q4. Which departments and major posts are covered?
faq.answer: Departments include Forensic Science Laboratory, Directorate of Education, NDMC, Information Technology Department, Department of Delhi Archives, and Labour Department. Major posts: TGT (Computer Science) — 675, Special Educator (Primary) — 450, IT Assistant Grade-A — 125, JSA (Cyber Forensic) — 81, plus many technical and teaching roles.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee and who is exempt?
faq.answer: Fee: ₹100 (paid via SBI e-Pay). Exemptions: Women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen. Fee is non‑refundable.

faq.question: Q6. What are the educational and skill requirements?
faq.answer: Qualifications vary by post: from Matric/ITI/NCVT (technical posts) to Graduation/Diploma and Master’s degrees (scientific/teaching posts). Several posts require typing/key‑depression speed (e.g., 8,000 key depressions/hour) or B.Ed/CTET/RCI credentials for teaching posts. Check post‑wise requirements in the notification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the age limit and are there relaxations?
faq.answer: Age limits vary by post (examples: JSA posts 18–27 yrs; most teaching posts up to 30 yrs; some technical posts up to 32–37 yrs). Age relaxations apply as per Central Government rules (SC/ST +5, OBC-Delhi +3, PwBD +10, Ex‑Servicemen service period +3, and others as specified).

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern and marking scheme?
faq.answer: Single-stage Computer Based Test (CBT) of 200 marks, 2 hours. Exam types: One Tier General, One Tier Technical, or One Tier Technical/Teaching depending on the post. Negative marking: 0.25 marks per wrong answer. Minimum qualifying marks: General/EWS 40%, OBC‑Delhi 35%, SC/ST/PwBD 30%.

faq.question: Q9. How will reservation and OBC-Delhi rules work?
faq.answer: Reservation for UR/OBC (Delhi)/SC/ST/EWS/PwBD/ESM/MSP as per GNCTD rules. Only OBC candidates with valid OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer) certificates issued for Delhi (on/after 01/04/2026 and not later than 15/07/2026) are eligible for OBC‑Delhi reservation. EWS / income certificates must follow notification timelines.

faq.question: Q10. What are the key application instructions and documents to keep ready?
faq.answer: Register once on dsssbonline.nic.in, then apply for relevant post code(s). Only one application per post code; multiple apps for same code may lead to rejection. Pay fee via SBI e‑Pay if applicable. Keep scanned recent passport photo, signature, educational certificates, caste/PwBD/EWS/ESM certificates, and experience documents ready for later e‑dossier upload. No corrections allowed after submission; save the application printout.
